title: Skill: okf-index ‚Äî OKF knowledge base builder (Confluence/web/docs/notes ‚Üí Obsidian vault + FTS5 search)
description:
## Goal

Build a self-contained Pi/Codex agent skill `okf-index` that ingests knowledge from multiple sources (Confluence, websites, local documents, own notes) into an [Open Knowledge Format v0.1](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/knowledge-catalog/tree/main/okf) bundle stored as plain markdown, browsable directly in Obsidian, with fast local search (SQLite FTS5 + tag facets) and always-on LLM enrichment. Telegram sources are explicitly Phase 2.

## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Skill lives at `okf-index/` IN THIS REPO (the working skills repo) and follows THIS REPO's `AGENTS.md`: short `SKILL.md` router + `references/` + `scripts/run.py` + `config/settings.json` + `evals/evals.json`; portable `uv run --script scripts/run.py`; `schema get --json`; semantic exit codes 0-5; `--help` shows current time; help that gives the agent time context.
- Produces OKF v0.1-conformant bundles: every concept is markdown + parseable YAML frontmatter with a non-empty `type`; supports `index.md` (progressive disclosure, bundle-root may carry `okf_version`), `log.md`, bundle-relative cross-links `/path/to/x.md`, and `# Citations`. Permissive consumption (never reject on unknown fields/types).
- Bundle = a plain directory that opens directly as an Obsidian vault; no proprietary format or required Obsidian plugins for basic browsing.
- CLI topology is noun-first `resource [subresource] action` with isolated connector resources: `confluence page ...`, `web crawl|fetch`, `doc ingest`, `note add`; plus `bundle build|index|validate|stats`, `search`, `tag list|show`; `auth status|setup|check|delete`; `doctor`; `schema get`.
- Confluence integration uses an embedded REST client inside `okf-index` (fully self-contained), with its own credentials in `config/settings.json` (keyring-first, env fallback) ‚Äî NOT a subprocess call to any external/sibling confluence skill.
- Web ingestion uses `crawl4ai` (`AsyncWebCrawler` + `BFSDeepCrawlStrategy` + `DefaultMarkdownGenerator`) with bounded `--max-depth`/`--max-pages` and an `--allowed-host` same-domain filter. (The usage pattern is borrowed as READ-ONLY inspiration from an external example skill; the code is implemented inside `okf-index` ‚Äî no link to that skill.)
- Local document ingestion uses `markitdown` to convert `.md/.txt/.pdf/.docx/.pptx/.html` to markdown uniformly.
- Every ingest runs an LLM enrichment pass (configured model) that produces `description`, `tags[]`, and cross-link suggestions; enrichment is the default path, not optional.
- Fast search via a local SQLite FTS5 index at `<vault>/.okf/index.db` over title+description+body+tags, plus a tags table for faceting; incremental rebuild keyed on `source_id+content_hash+mtime`. `.okf/` is gitignored.
- Bundle location is the `OKF_VAULT_PATH` setting (default `~/okf-vault`). The skill NEVER auto-inits or auto-commits git; it only writes files and may print a `git status` hint.
- All reads are bounded (`--limit`, `--fields`, filters); all mutations support `--dry-run --json` then `--yes --json`.

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] `uv run --script scripts/run.py schema get --json` returns a complete, valid schema for every implemented command.
- [ ] `okf-index doctor --json` validates settings, checks keyring/env sources (no values leaked), probes Confluence + enrich model, with semantic exit codes.
- [ ] End-to-end: ingesting a Confluence page, a web page, a local `.docx`, and a note each yields an OKF-conformant concept file (frontmatter + `type`) inside `OKF_VAULT_PATH`, with LLM-generated description/tags.
- [ ] `bundle validate --json` confirms OKF v0.1 conformance (SPEC ¬ß9) for the produced bundle.
- [ ] `search "<q>" --tag <t> --type <t> --limit N --json` returns ranked, bounded results from the FTS5 index in <100ms for a 1k-concept bundle.
- [ ] `tag list --json` and `tag show <tag> --json` work off the tags table.
- [ ] `bundle index --rebuild|--since` performs incremental reindexing correctly.
- [ ] The bundle directory opens in Obsidian as a normal vault with working internal links and tag pane.
- [ ] `auth status|setup|check|delete` lifecycle works for Confluence + model credentials; `auth status` never prints secret values.
- [ ] `evals/evals.json` has atomic evals for every implemented command; enrich-related evals assert STRUCTURE (frontmatter present, `type` set, conformance), not exact LLM wording.
- [ ] All unit tests + `python3 -m py_compile` green; no secrets in repo; no `curl`/direct HTTP outside the CLI.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ‚ùå NO direct HTTP/curl outside `scripts/run.py` (AGENTS.md: all logic via the CLI).
- ‚ùå NO storing tokens/model keys in the skill folder or `settings.json` (credentials: keyring ‚Üí env only).
- ‚ùå NO non-conformant OKF output: a concept without frontmatter or without `type` is a release blocker (SPEC ¬ß9).
- ‚ùå NO subprocess calls to any external/sibling confluence skill (Q2 decision: embedded client).
- ‚ùå NO auto-init/auto-commit of git by the skill (Q4 decision: git is manual; skill only writes files).
- ‚ùå NO unbounded search/list (every read bounded by `--limit`/filters).
- ‚ùå NO hardcoded LLM provider/model (Q3: configured via `config/settings.json`).
- ‚ùå NO silent LLM calls without a configured provider surfaced in `doctor`.
- ‚ùå NO mocking OKF conformance in `bundle validate` tests (must assert real SPEC ¬ß9 rules).
- ‚ùå NO rewriting the FTS5 choice (Q1: SQLite FTS5 + tags is the search core).
- ‚ùå NO references/links/imports/subprocess/hardcoded paths to the external example-skills repo (`.../AI/skills`) or ANY sibling skill. Those repos are READ-ONLY pattern inspiration ONLY (Confluence-client approach, crawl4ai usage, skill structure). Needed logic MUST be copied into `okf-index`; the skill must run standalone with zero dependency on any other skill's files.

## Approach

Noun-first CLI with isolated connector resources (`confluence`, `web`, `doc`, `note`) feeding a shared OKF pipeline: `connector ‚Üí markdown ‚Üí OKF writer (frontmatter+body) ‚Üí enrich (LLM: description/tags/links) ‚Üí write .md + update index.md/log.md ‚Üí FTS5 indexer ‚Üí search`. The pipeline is shared so adding a source (e.g. Telegram in Phase 2) is one connector module + one `ingest` mapping, not a new vertical. Settings/auth/doctor reuse the AGENTS.md unified-manifest pattern. Enrichment is always-on via a configured OpenAI-compatible or Gemini model; evals assert structure, not LLM prose.

## Architecture

- `scripts/run.py` ‚Äî single PEP 723 launcher (deps: crawl4ai, playwright, markitdown, keyring, google-genai or openai/litellm). Layers: settings/auth ‚Üí connectors ‚Üí OKF writer/validator ‚Üí enrich ‚Üí indexer ‚Üí search.
- `config/settings.json` ‚Äî manifest: `OKF_VAULT_PATH` (non-secret default), `OKF_ENRICH_PROVIDER`/`OKF_ENRICH_MODEL` (non-secret), `GEMINI_API_KEY`/`OPENAI_API_KEY` (credential), `CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL` (non-secret), `CONFLUENCE_USERNAME`+`CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN` (credential).
- Bundle layout under `OKF_VAULT_PATH`: `<source>/<concept>.md`, auto `index.md` per dir, root `log.md`, `.okf/index.db` (gitignored).
- Frontmatter producer keys beyond SPEC: `source`, `source_id`, `content_hash`, `okf_version`.

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Knowledge is scattered across Confluence, websites, local docs, and personal notes with no unified, portable, searchable, agent-friendly store. OKF v0.1 gives a vendor-neutral markdown+frontmatter standard; Obsidian already speaks that format. The gap is a producer+consumer agent skill that fills an OKF bundle from many sources and searches it fast.

### Research Findings
**OKF (SPEC v0.1):**
- Concept = markdown + YAML frontmatter; `type` REQUIRED; recommended `title, description, resource, tags, timestamp`.
- `index.md` (no frontmatter except bundle-root `okf_version`), `log.md` (ISO date sections), bundle-relative links `/path/x.md`, `# Citations`.
- Permissive: consumers MUST NOT reject on unknown types/fields/broken links (SPEC ¬ß9). Conformance = parseable frontmatter + non-empty `type` + reserved-filename rules.

**confluence skill (external example, READ-ONLY inspiration):** `uv run --script scripts/run.py`, `page get/search/section/toc/tree/attachment/diagram`, bearer auth keyring‚Üíenv, `--dry-run/--yes`, `CONFLUENCE_AUTO_CONFIRM`. (We do NOT call or import it; we implement an equivalent client inside `okf-index`.)

**sandbox + crawl4ai (external example, READ-ONLY inspiration):** `AsyncWebCrawler`+`BrowserConfig`+`CrawlerRunConfig`+`BFSDeepCrawlStrategy`+`FilterChain(URLPatternFilter,ContentTypeFilter)`, `DefaultMarkdownGenerator`‚Üímarkdown, playwright chromium, deep crawl with `max_depth`/`include_external=False`. (Usage pattern borrowed by reading; implementation lives inside `okf-index`.)

**AGENTS.md (THIS REPO ‚Äî authoritative rules source):** SKILL.md 50-100 line router; references/runtime|safety|workflows|api; `schema get --json`; `auth status/setup/check/delete`; atomic `doctor`; unified `config/settings.json` manifest (keyring for credentials, env for non-secret overrides); `evals/evals.json`; semantic exit codes; help with current time. NOTE: other repos carry their own (possibly older) copy of AGENTS.md ‚Äî always follow THIS repo's copy.

**Environment checks:** system Python ships SQLite WITH FTS5 (verified); `markitdown==0.1.6` on PyPI; beads_rust `br` is the issue tracker.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Source-connector resources + shared OKF pipeline (CHOSEN) ‚úì
Isolated `confluence`/`web`/`doc`/`note` resources feeding one shared writer/enricher/indexer. Noun-first, explorable, matches AGENTS.md. Adding sources = one module. Chosen for consistency with sibling skills and clean schema/help.

#### 2. Unified `ingest --from <source>` ‚ùå
One `ingest` verb with `--from`. Rejected: mixes very different argument shapes behind one verb, weaker schema/help, harder to bound per-source.

#### 3. Declarative `source add` + `bundle build` from a manifest ‚ùå
Reproducible `sources.yaml`, but adds indirection unneeded for interactive agent use. Reuse the idea later as an optional recipe layer, not the core.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope (Phase 1):** Confluence (embedded client), web (crawl4ai), local docs (markitdown md/txt/pdf/docx/pptx/html), own notes; OKF writer/validator; always-on LLM enrich; FTS5 index + tag facets + search; auth/doctor/settings; evals; Obsidian-compatible output.
**Out of scope (Phase 2+):** Telegram; declarative sources manifest; OKF `viz.html` graph viewer; vector/semantic search; account-linking across sources.

### Open Questions
- Exact Gemini vs OpenAI-compatible default for enrich (decide in Task 2; default Gemini to mirror OKF reference_agent).
- Concept path/slug conventions per source (decide in Task 1 core).
- Enrichment prompt + how to present suggested links for human approval (decide in Task 2).
